trail.val: FY26 reaffirmed and FY27 midpoint opportunity held - both EXCLUDE pending Cogentrix + Meta PJM-nuclear PPAs

forward.note: Guidance excludes Cogentrix + Meta PPAs to be folded in post-close; ~$700-750M potential FY27 add.

trail.val: >$12.50 adj FCF-before-growth/share FY26; ~$16 w/ Cogentrix + Meta operating PPAs; $22-25 illustrative if 2026-30 cash all to buybacks
line of sight >$10B cash gen 2026-2027 (~$3B equity, ~$4B growth, ~$3B unallocated) at ~2.3x net debt/EBITDA by YE27

forward.note: Aggressive buyback into high FCF yield is core to the bull; 10b5-1 designed to accelerate on weakness.

Cogentrix on track to close 2H26 - triggers FY26 guidance + FY27 midpoint update

forward.note: Close is the catalyst that resets guidance upward; ~$500M EBITDA run-rate add.

trail.val: ~3.8GW nuclear under 20-yr PPAs: Amazon Comanche Peak (energize 2027, full ramp 2032) + Meta 2,176MW operating + 433MW uprates (online 2031-2034)

forward.note: Contracted nuclear de-risks the model toward ~half of EBITDA from stable sources once PPAs ramp.

trail.val: ~3.2GW more nuclear (Beaver Valley + Comanche Peak) uncontracted; appetite 'as high as ever'; meaningful DC tightening not until 'late 2027/early 2028'
ERCOT total growth ~30-40GW by 2030 (~10-15GW DC); load 'real but not at extremely elevated levels'; deals 'complicated' and slow
Jefferies HOLD - IPPs ceding DC-contracting race to regulated utilities/BTM; prefers NRG/NI/ETR

forward.note: Mgmt more cautious on pace than the market; Jefferies sees IPPs losing share to regulated/BTM - the central contested point.

trail.val: ERCOT 5-6% and PJM 2-3% to 2030; fleet only ~60% utilized = operating leverage
JPM models 138 GW US DC capacity growth through 2030 (up from 122 GW); power the binding constraint

forward.note: Higher utilization of existing fleet without greenfield risk is the operating-leverage thesis; forwards don't yet reflect mgmt's own view.
